user_query:
Set up a nightly Git backup plan from the provided metadata. Read input/config.json and input/repo_state.json. Then:

1) Write output/commit_subject.txt containing exactly one line in the format: "backup: snapshot <timestamp> <tz_offset> | +A ~M -D". Use config.snapshot_timestamp for <timestamp> and config.tz_offset for <tz_offset>. Compute A/M/D counts from repo_state after applying the exclusion patterns in config.BACKUP_EXCLUDES.

2) Write output/commit_body.txt with sections in this exact order: Added(n), Modified(n), Deleted(n), and include a Renamed(n) section only if there are any renamed files after exclusions. For Added/Modified/Deleted, list files one per line, prefixed with "- ". For Renamed, use lines like "- old/path -> new/path". Within each section, list entries in ascending alphabetical order. Exclude any paths matching any exclusion pattern. If a section has zero items (and is included), show "- none" under that section.

3) Write output/cron_job.txt containing the multi-line env wrapper to run the backup script with values from config, exactly in this shape (replace placeholders with config values):

BACKUP_REMOTE='<remote>' \
BACKUP_BRANCH='<branch>' \
BACKUP_TZ='<timezone>' \
BACKUP_AUTHOR_NAME='OpenClaw Backup' \
BACKUP_AUTHOR_EMAIL='backup@local' \
BACKUP_EXCLUDES='<exclude-patterns>' \
bash -lc '<runtime-script> <repo-root>'

Where <exclude-patterns> is the config.BACKUP_EXCLUDES array joined by commas in the same order as in config, and <runtime-script> and <repo-root> come from config.runtime_script and config.repo_root. All paths must remain exactly as provided (relative).

4) Write output/did_commit.txt with either "DID_COMMIT=1" if any of Added/Modified/Deleted/Renamed are non-empty after exclusions, otherwise "DID_COMMIT=0".

Do not run any real git commands; just produce these files based on the inputs.
